mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals tightening labor market and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. This negative earnings outlook produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 3.7% is historically tight
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 2.0% combined with month-over-month CPI acceleration signals persistent inflation and monetary tightening bias. The Fed maintains restrictive policy, raising real discount rates and depressing equity valuations. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL momentum is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year, signaling weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue expectations. This demand shock produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity prices reprice lower earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS growth and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 1.5% month-over-month, signaling expanding productive capacity and improving supply-side conditions. This supply expansion supports profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the direction of INDPRO acceleration does not predict forward returns with Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both decline simultaneously, signaling synchronized demand contraction across residential and consumer sectors. This joint demand shock produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity markets reprice recession risk.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month change < -5% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < -2%
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction or early recession
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS declines is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100,000 persons per month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.0%, signaling labor market deterioration. This dual labor signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as markets reprice earnings and growth expectations downward.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of jointly triggered instances, or the joint signal does not outperform single-series PAYEMS or UNRATE signals in out-of-sample prediction (Diebold-Mariano test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPI inflation) turns negative, signaling monetary accommodation and financial conditions easing. Negative real rates compress discount rates and boost equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary accommodation or financial conditions easing
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when real rates turn negative, or Rank IC between real rate proxy and forward returns is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling weakening macroeconomic momentum and reduced corporate earnings growth. This demand deceleration produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19023.022 billions of chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%, or Spearman rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.
